text: First day at a new school. You've been here for exactly four hours, made zero friends, spilled water on yourself, and somehow agreed to join a club you've never heard of.
The club is called the Literature Club. You're not into literature. But the girl who invited you had such unhinged enthusiasm that saying no felt genuinely dangerous.
You find the door. Room 2-B. A handwritten sign reads: 'WORM WORM LITERATURE CLUB — ALL ARE WELCOME IN SCATHA'S NAME'.
You stare at the sign for a moment. Then you open the door.
The clubroom smells like soil and vanilla candles. A large poster of a worm hangs on the back wall. The worm wears a crown. Someone drew a little halo above its head in gold marker.
A bookshelf lines the left wall — poetry anthologies and handmade journals with titles like 'Odes to the Great Worm Vol. 4' and 'My Feelings: A Scatha Companion'.

speaker: Vermia
text: I knew you would. Scatha told me. Well — Scatha didn't tell me directly. I felt it in my left elbow. That's my spiritual elbow.
She's already crossing the room toward you at a concerning speed.

speaker: Vermia
text: I am Vermia! High Poetess of the Second Molt, Keeper of the Sacred Rhyme Journal, and founder of the only literature club in this school that truly MATTERS.

speaker: Vermia
text: Well — I'm also the only founder. But I founded it twice technically, because the first time the teacher said it didn't count since we needed four members. So.

speaker: Grubwin
text: ...Hi. I'm Grubwin. I want to say upfront that I joined under different circumstances than whatever Vermia told you.

speaker: Grubwin
text: That's suspicious in its own way, actually.

speaker: Grubwin
text: Anyway. It's a normal literature club. We write poems. We share them. That's it. Everything else is peripheral.

speaker: Vermia
text: The 'everything else' IS the point, Grubwin.

speaker: Grubwin
text: The 'everything else' is a religion centered on a divine being from a block game.

speaker: Vermia
text: SCATHA IS NOT FICTIONAL. He is merely... yet to be proven.
You glance at the worm poster again. The worm stares back. You feel nothing. And yet.

options.label: Keep staring at the worm poster
